---
title: "BAA1104: E-Portfolio and Business Analytics Challenges - Reflection"
---

## Looking Back on the Module

This page reflects on my experience completing the ePortfolio module as part of my 
MSc in Business Analytics at DCU. It covers the skills I developed, the challenges 
I faced, and how this experience has shaped my thinking as an emerging data professional.

---

## 🧠 What I Learned

When I first encountered the technical requirements of this module — Quarto, Git, 
GitHub Pages, executable Python code — I found it genuinely difficult to follow. Not 
just the syntax or the tools themselves, but understanding the *why* behind everything. 
Why do we use version control? Why does the folder structure matter? Why does a 
`.nojekyll` file need to exist at all?

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
It was only through repeated practice — breaking things, fixing them, and breaking 
them again — that the logic behind each step began to make sense. That process of 
trial and error turned out to be the most effective teacher.
:::

Over time, I went from struggling to preview a single page locally to building and 
deploying a fully structured, multi-page portfolio site with live Python code. That 
progression, however gradual, represents real technical growth.

---

## 💡 What Surprised Me

The biggest surprise of this module was realising that I could build and own my own 
professional website — one that I control completely, can update at any time, and 
that genuinely represents who I am and what I can do.

Before this module, a personal website felt like something only developers had. Now 
I understand it as a professional tool — as important as a CV, and in many ways more 
powerful. It is a living document that grows with me.

::: {.callout-tip}
The idea that this portfolio is not something I submit and forget, but something I 
can continue to build on throughout my career, genuinely changed how I think about 
professional development.
:::

---

## 🏫 The Classes

I found the classes throughout this module to be genuinely interactive and engaging. 
Rather than passively receiving information, we were encouraged to try things, make 
mistakes, and figure out solutions — which suited me well as an analytics beginner.

There is something valuable about working through a problem in a classroom setting 
where everyone is at a similar level. It made the technical challenges feel less 
intimidating and more like puzzles to solve together.

---

## 🔄 What I Would Do Differently

If I were starting this module again, I would invest time upfront in short online 
courses to build foundational knowledge before the semester began. Understanding 
the basics of Git, the command line, and how Python environments work would have 
saved significant time and frustration in the early weeks.

| Area | What I'd Do Differently |
|---|---|
| Git & Version Control | Complete a beginner Git course before week one |
| Python Environment | Set up and test Jupyter/Quarto before the first class |
| Project Structure | Plan the folder structure from day one rather than reorganising later |
| Time Management | Commit smaller, more regularly rather than large end-of-session pushes |

---

## 🔗 Connecting to My Future

I am building a career that sits at the intersection of accountancy and data analytics. 
This module has shown me that technical skills like these are not just useful for 
data scientists — they are increasingly expected of anyone working with data in a 
professional context.

::: {.callout-tip}
As a part-qualified ACCA accountant, I now see this portfolio as a bridge between 
my financial expertise and my growing analytics capability. It demonstrates not just 
what I know, but how I think and how I work.
:::

Going forward, I intend to keep this portfolio updated as I complete my ACCA 
qualification, develop new projects, and grow in my role at the Mercantile Group. 
It is no longer just a college submission — it is the beginning of my professional 
digital presence.

---

## 📊 Skills Developed in This Module

| Skill | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Quarto | Never heard of it | Can build and deploy a full site |
| Git & GitHub | Basic awareness | Regular commits, branching, GitHub Pages |
| Python in documents | Code only in notebooks | Executable chunks in published pages |
| Web publishing | No experience | Live site on GitHub Pages |
| Professional portfolio | CV only | Multi-page portfolio with live projects |

---

::: {.callout-note}
Thank you for reading. You can explore my projects on the [Projects page](projects/index.qmd) 
or [get in touch](contact.qmd) if you'd like to connect.
:::
